title: mfa — cache keys + tags
description: MFA caches split into two categories. EPHEMERAL STATE (enrolment previews, WebAuthn registration challenges, verify challenge outcomes) is Redis-only — never persisted to Postgres, TTL-purged aggressively. LOOKUP CACHES (per-Identity credential lists) are Redis with a short TTL for read-heavy paths (login MFA, /me/mfa summary). Every regulated_secret is NEVER cached (see the never_cached list at the bottom).

keys.description: Round-1 TOTP enrolment preview. Stores a signed envelope containing the ephemeral TOTP secret (base32) + issued_at timestamp + expires_at. Consumed atomically by round-2 confirm; deleted on TTL if never confirmed.
keys.encryption_note: The envelope IS the plaintext TOTP secret — this Redis key MUST be encrypted at rest (Redis TLS + at-rest encryption on the Redis persistence layer). If the Redis provider does not support at-rest encryption, this cache MUST NOT be used — an alternative session-side envelope is required.

keys.description: Round-1 WebAuthn registration challenge. Stores the 32-byte random challenge sent to navigator.credentials.create() so it can be verified in round 2.
Login-time WebAuthn assertion challenge. Same shape as the registration challenge; separate key namespace so the two lifecycles don't collide.

Cached active-credentials list for an Identity. Read on every WebAuthn assertion (login) + /me/mfa/webauthn/credentials list. Very short TTL so a credential disable / delete propagates within one minute.

Cached MFA-posture summary (totp_enrolled + webauthn_credentials_count + recovery_codes_remaining). Feeds the /me/mfa GET response + the `mfa.require_challenge_complete` middleware's `Identity.mfa_enabled` accessor.

Rolling window of MfaChallengeFailed events for the MfaSuspiciousActivityDetector. Uses Redis sorted-set with score=timestamp; window size = `config('mfa.suspicious_activity.window_minutes')`. Read on every MfaChallengeFailed to compute the threshold check.
Debounce guard preventing repeat LowRecoveryCodesRemainingNotification within 30 days (per-event debounce; the nightly sweep has its own broader debounce).
Cooldown timer set when a challenge exhausts max_attempts. Blocks fresh challenge issuance for `config('mfa.challenge.cooldown_minutes_after_max')`.

never_cached: Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted (plaintext form — even the KMS-decrypted plaintext stays in a single stack frame during verification, never travels through Redis)
Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed (individual bcrypt hashes — the per-Identity constant-time compare loop reads directly from Postgres)
WebauthnCredential.public_key (bytea — verifier reads directly from Postgres per assertion)
MfaEnrollmentPreview.totp_secret (base32) — held only in the mfa:enroll:preview:{session_id} envelope, which IS the cache but is a controlled encrypted-at-rest region
RecoveryCodesDownload.codes (plaintext array — emitted in one response, never persisted to cache OR log OR metric)
notes: The mfa:enroll:preview:{session_id} key is the ONLY cache that carries plaintext credential material. Its encryption-at-rest is a compliance requirement, not a nice-to-have. Deployments that can't guarantee encryption-at-rest on Redis MUST configure `config('mfa.totp.preview_backend')` to `session` (falls back to Laravel's encrypted session driver) — a per-tenant Doppler policy decision.

Suspicious-activity + notification-debounce keys are intentionally scoped to the Identity ULID because they carry policy state (rate-limiter data). Cardinality is bounded by the number of Identities with MFA enrolled; this is fine at every scale we anticipate.
